rules.description: Boundary: PR lifecycle write work must go through the pr skill / pr-step workflow, not raw GitHub CLI `gh pr create`.

deny.reason: The user has set a PR lifecycle boundary: direct `gh pr create ...` is a PR write action and is not an agent workflow.
deny.redirect: Invoke `/pr` or the `pr` skill and use `pr-step`; pr-step is self-sufficient since #598 and owns PR creation, review, checks, CodeRabbit handling, merge, and cleanup.

rules.description: Boundary: the deploy-owned Token-OS live runtime must not be unlocked for direct agent edits.

deny.reason: The Token-OS live runtime (~/runtimes/Token-OS/live) is intentionally write-locked. This is an ARCHITECTURE CONSTRAINT set by the user, not a bug to work around: workers must not unlock deploy-owned runtime paths for direct edits.
deny.redirect: Make the change in a worktree and ship it through PR/CD instead: `worktree-setup <branch> --project Token-OS`, edit there, then push and run `pr-step` / the `pr` skill. Do not retry the unlock or look for another path to the same write; escalate if in-place runtime maintenance is genuinely required.

rules.description: Boundary: root-wide NAS searches must use the lag-aware nas-grep wrapper or narrower repo-local search.

deny.reason: Root-wide NAS search is a user-defined performance boundary. Raw recursive search across /Volumes, /Volumes/Imperium, /Volumes/Civic, /mnt/imperium, or $IMPERIUM can wedge the NAS/Mac and should not be run directly.
deny.redirect: Use `nas-grep <pattern> <NAS-path>` for NAS-root scans, or narrow the root to `$IMPERIUM/Imperium-ENV/<subdir>`. For Git repositories, prefer `git grep <pattern> -- <paths>` or repo-local `rg` from the checkout. Non-filesystem stdin filtering remains fine.

rules.description: Boundary: raw tmux mutating commands bypass Token-OS pane identity, typing guards, dispatch, and tmuxctl invariants.

deny.reason: Raw mutating `tmux` commands cross the Token-OS pane-control boundary. They bypass tmuxctl, typing guards, dispatch identity, and the agent prompt-delivery wrappers.
deny.redirect: Use the sanctioned wrapper for the intent: `agent-cmd`/`talk`/`brief` for live agent text, `dispatch --target mechanicus:new` for new workers, `tmuxctl stack ...` for managed-stack changes, or `tmux-mark-for-close` / `instance-stop` for teardown. Read-only tmux inspection such as capture/list/display remains allowed.
